pi_items.source: Specified in task.json and verified by examining setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts

pi_items.verified_value: 12.1 RPM (rated rotor speed of NREL 5MW), but 9 RPM is a valid sub-rated operating point
pi_items.source: Web search of NREL 5MW specifications: https://docs.nrel.gov/docs/fy09osti/38060.pdf and multiple NREL Forum discussions. Rated rotor speed is 12.1 rpm, not 9 rpm.
NREL 5MW specifications show cut-in wind speed of 3 m/s and rated wind speed of 11.4 m/s. Using 5 m/s as minimum is consistent with standard practice.
NREL 5MW specifications confirm cut-out wind speed of 25 m/s. https://forums.nrel.gov/t/nrel5mw-rotor-speed-data/867

The NREL 5MW has a rated wind speed of 11.4 m/s at its rated rotor speed of 12.1 rpm. At a fixed, sub-rated speed of 9 rpm, the power curve would peak at a lower wind speed due to aerodynamic stall. While 10.0 m/s is physically plausible as a peak for fixed 9 rpm operation, I could not find specific experimental or simulation data confirming this exact value.
This is a task parameter for acceptable deviation from expected peak wind speed. No external reference available.
Verified in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. Expected at /home/ga/Documents/projects/stall_simulation_data.txt
Verified in setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, and verifier.py. Expected at /home/ga/Documents/projects/stall_report.txt

pi_items.verified_value: NREL 5MW Reference Offshore Wind Turbine (real, documented model)
pi_items.source: Web search confirming NREL 5MW as a well-documented reference turbine. https://docs.nrel.gov/docs/fy09osti/38060.pdf
NREL 5MW specifications consistently reference 5 MW rated power output
NREL specifications from technical report NREL/TP-500-38060 and forum discussions

privileged_info_summary: The NREL 5MW Reference Wind Turbine is a real, publicly documented offshore wind turbine model with a rated rotor speed of 12.1 rpm (not the 9 rpm specified in the task), a rated wind speed of 11.4 m/s, cut-in wind speed of 3 m/s, cut-out wind speed of 25 m/s, and rated power output of 5 MW. The task simulates the turbine at a fixed sub-rated rotor speed of 9 rpm over a wind speed range of 5-25 m/s to observe stall behavior and determine peak power characteristics. The expected peak wind speed of approximately 10 m/s is plausible for fixed 9 rpm operation (below the rated 12.1 rpm), as stall effects would limit power at higher wind speeds.
